title: Night of the Living Dead
summaries: "Night of the Living Dead" by George A. Romero is a full-length film script that falls within the horror genre and was written in the late 20th century. The film is notable for its groundbreaking approach to the zombie apocalypse narrative, exploring themes of survival, societal collapse, and human nature in the face of a terrifying threat.   The storyline follows a group of individuals who become trapped in a farmhouse as the world around them descends into chaos due to a zombie outbreak. As the undead converge, the characters must confront not only the external menace but also their own fears, differences, and conflicts. The film intricately weaves a sense of dread and urgency, showcasing how societal norms break down under extreme duress, ultimately leading to a dramatic and unsettling climax. The characters' interactions reveal deeper themes of distrust, desperation, and the struggle for power in dire situations, making it a seminal work in the horror genre.
